top_issues.headline: 34 content areas are not inside a landmark region
top_issues.user_impact: Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot reach this content using shortcut keys.
top_issues.suggestion: Wrap all page content in appropriate landmark elements: <header>, <nav>, <main>, <aside>, <footer>. Content outside landmarks is invisible to landmark navigation.
top_issues.example_before: Career

top_issues.headline: 3 links have no accessible name
top_issues.user_impact: Screen reader users cannot determine where a link goes. Voice control users cannot click links by name. This fails basic WCAG Level A compliance.
top_issues.suggestion: Every link must have a descriptive accessible name. Add visible text between the anchor tags, or add an aria-label if the link contains only an icon. Avoid 'click here' or 'read more' as the sole link text.
top_issues.example_before:
<a href="https://github.com/tayyabataimur" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="p-1.5 text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 dark:hover:text-gray-300 transition-colors">
<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/tayyaba-taimur/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="p-1.5 text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 dark:hover:text-gray-300 transition-colors">
<a href="mailto:tayyabataimur96@gmail.com" class="p-1.5 text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 dark:hover:text-gray-300 transition-colors">

top_issues.headline: Page is missing a main landmark
top_issues.user_impact: Keyboard and screen reader users cannot skip to the main content. This is a common navigation barrier for users who visit many pages on your site.
top_issues.suggestion: Wrap the primary page content in a <main> element. Every page should have exactly one <main>.
